key_insight: G(Wa)NN is a deep-learning model that predicts spin-orbit-coupled electronic Hamiltonians directly in an orthogonal Wannier basis, enabling scalable quantum transport workflows.
engineering_relevance: This can accelerate large-scale materials and device simulations by combining accurate Hamiltonian generation with linear-scaling post-processing for systems beyond 10K atoms.
method: Trained on 111K+ Wannier Hamiltonians spanning 69 elements
Uses a universal model plus local finetuning for custom datasets
Paired with Tailwater for automated subspace projection and KPM-based spectral calculations
evidence_signal: The abstract reports training on an unprecedented dataset of more than 111K Wannier Hamiltonians and 150M+ hopping matrices across 69 elements.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and compatibility of the target Wannierization/projection workflow, so adapting to new materials likely requires careful validation.
paper_title: Towards a universal model for spin-orbit coupled Wannier Hamiltonians
